title: Chapter One Hundred: A Spirit Pretending
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

The words landed like a blade between his ribs.

*I'm not the only one.*

The Divine Sword Group Leader's eyes were piercing as they bored into him. Though his tone was casual, his gaze struck like lightning. The boy immediately sensed the weight behind the words. They were not idle remarks.

He had seen through it. Thoroughly. All while speaking casually, while bleeding from internal wounds that would have killed a lesser man.

*Lightning Genius would die young.*

The Divine Sword Group Leader had realized it.

*This could happen.* Yeon-shin did not believe the Group Leader's internal martial arts were inferior to the Zhongnan Sword Immortal's. Just as that old immortal had instantly discerned his constitution, it was not surprising that this man could perceive his ill-fated destiny. He had even brushed his hand across the crown of his head -- the Hundred Meetings point.

Yeon-shin glanced at his grandfather. Ma Yeon-jeok's martial prowess had diminished in later years, the result of cultivating the Desolate Ma family's divine technique with its distinct peak period. And because of past karma, the old man had never laid a hand on his grandson. Even the Desolate Fortress Lord had remained silent, leaving no one to recognize the early-death constitution.

"The Dragon Family youngster's words aren't entirely wrong," Ma Yeon-jeok said, meeting his gaze. "You should pay attention to your body."

*So the Group Leader was from the Dragon Family.*

Ma Yeon-jeok smiled in agreement with the Group Leader's words. "Yeon-shin, you truly are the treasure of the main fortress. Rising talents like you, just emerging and making a name for themselves, are the future of every sect."

"Former leader, surely you don't mean to--"

The Group Leader frowned. His words carried an entirely different meaning. A conversation that looked like agreement but was two men speaking in separate rooms -- Ma Yeon-jeok talking about politics and the Group Leader talking about death.

"Even as martial artists, we cannot avoid life-and-death battles," the old man continued, bulging eyes earnest. "But life must still be prioritized. Not simply because Yeon-shin is my grandson. Whatever the mission, the main fortress should actively support him--"

"This kind of talk -- it truly feels like senility. To think the former leader was once the goal I aspired to reach."

"Foolish words! You're just a reckless fool."

The Group Leader turned his head. His pale eyes, brimming with restrained energy, seemed to question what on earth was happening. Yeon-shin met his gaze silently, then lowered his head. A slight nod.

*I know what you're saying. I have always known.*

The Group Leader clicked his tongue like an old man and shook his head. "My, what level of karma has our elder accumulated..."

Ma Yeon-jeok remained unaware. If he learned of Yeon-shin's constitution -- would his devoted attitude change? Or would he desperately seek a way to save his grandson?

*Even having obtained a fragment of the World Tree's fruit from the Lord... No. It doesn't matter.*

There was no reason to get involved in such troublesome questions. Advancement could not be achieved through another's efforts. Realm breakthroughs were solitary.

"Lightning Genius, come here. Let's talk privately."

The Group Leader wiped blood from the back of his hand. His complexion was pale. He beckoned, and Ma Yeon-jeok stepped forward, expression dark.

"Dragon family boy. You're not going to make a rash proposal?"

"Your worries are severe, fitting for your senility." A brief smile. "Seeing how Ma Jin treasures this child, I don't want to hear that eunuch's curses if I take him rashly."

"What did you say?"

"You think we'll recruit Lightning Genius into the Divine Sword Squad, but we don't need him right now. A needless worry. Perhaps it's because the Squad was weak during your time as leader."

"What did you say?" Ma Yeon-jeok's face twisted with anger.

"You're angry. So -- Lightning Genius. Let's go. Just the two of us."

Yeon-shin followed.

---

Far from the others. A stretch of broken trail overlooking the valley, the landslide's dust still drifting in the distance. The Group Leader sat on a flat stone and breathed carefully. Each exhale had a rattle to it.

"You already know what I'm going to say."

"Yes."

"Then I'll be direct." His scholar-gentle eyes were gone. What remained was the gaze of a man who had killed three supreme masters in a single afternoon. "Your Hundred Meetings point is abnormal. The energy that gathers there is not human in the way I understand human. The Zhongnan Sword Immortal saw it, didn't he?"

"He did."

"And said nothing useful, I imagine."

Yeon-shin was silent.

"That's how those orthodox types are." The Group Leader looked at the sky. "Lightning Genius. You are a vessel that burns too fast. What flows through your meridians should sustain you for decades. Instead, it is consuming you. You feel it."

He did feel it. Had always felt it. The quiet erosion beneath the brilliance -- the price paid each time he activated his upper dantian, each time qi surged through the Hundred Meetings point. Like a forge that burned so bright it ate its own walls.

"How long?" Yeon-shin asked.

The Group Leader studied him. "That's not something I can answer with certainty. I'm a swordsman, not a physician. But I will say this -- the way you fight, the way you push yourself, you are shortening it."

"I know."

"Do you?" A hard edge entered the Group Leader's voice. "Because what I saw inside your meridians today was a boy who had made peace with dying. And that is a waste I will not tolerate."

Silence stretched between them. Wind carried dust from the collapsed peaks.

"The technique you stole from me," the Group Leader said, and a faint smile returned. "The pathway for single-strike explosive power. Use it. Refine it. It will help with your Brilliant River's rebound -- and it may, incidentally, reduce the strain on that point at your crown. Efficiency, Lightning Genius. You cannot afford waste."

"...Thank you."

"Don't thank me. Survive long enough that I can fight you properly one day."

He said it lightly. The way the crown prince had once said *let's meet often.* The way men said *don't die* without saying the words.

Yeon-shin bowed once and returned to the group.

---

Deep beneath the earth, in a hall where night-luminous pearls were strung like lamps, two figures walked.

The man's white hair shimmered in the fading glow. With each step he took, the nearest pearl flickered and died -- darkness spreading from him like breath from a great demon's mouth, swallowing everything in its wake. His languid, enchanting voice filled the corridor.

"New additions again. I should have them all removed. The Tang Family people particularly like brightness, but it's such an ill-fitting habit. Perhaps it's because they're so consumed by their own darkness."

"..."

"Seventh Apostle, why so quiet? You've been like this ever since you received salvation from this Sect Leader."

"I have much to think about," the woman answered briefly.

The Blood Flame Sect Leader laughed. "Was the life-threatening crisis too shocking? Come to think of it, you were raised precious within our sect. Surrounded by supreme masters like the purple ones from Desolate Fortress. You should take pride. Don't be intimidated."

His footwork was entrancing, containing profound principles. The Seventh Apostle's steps were light -- almost weightless.

"If only this body of mine were intact, I would have claimed the Divine Sword Group Leader's head. A precious opportunity, lost. It's fortunate the Tyrant Sword's master follows my will, though it's limited to a single use. Truly regrettable."

"..."

"I hope the Namgung Family Head at least took one of the Group Leader's arms."

"Please tell me that result too." The Seventh Apostle's voice carried sudden urgency. "You must."

The Blood Flame Sect Leader's laughter deepened, satisfied. "I know you care for the young Great Teacher. I shall tell you -- he is a boy who must be brought in someday."

Their footsteps echoed through the dark hall. Deeper. At the far end, a harsh elderly voice rang out.

"I've told you a hundred times not to disturb this old one's cultivation."

A large stone chamber. An old man holding a small dagger glared at them, thin body draped in gold-gleaming silk robes. The final night pearl flickered dimly, its light twisting as it caught his eyes and the short blade in his hand.

In the centre of the room stood a stone bed with high legs. Tied to it, eyes wide, was a Blood Demon Swordmaster with dark red hair. The dagger's tip barely touched the creature's chest.

"Disturbing your cultivation? How could that be? When I'm the one who promised full support?"

The Blood Flame Sect Leader dropped the figure from his shoulder onto the floor.

"What did you--" The old man's expression shifted from disbelief to ecstasy. "With this one, the great method will be greatly advanced."

He crouched by the captive's head, muttering. "I was only wanting to reference a body that has reached its peak through proper methods. It wouldn't do to dissect your own, Sect Leader. Your blood demons' regenerative abilities have opened new paths in medicine -- paths that can't be described in words."

"You please me with your audacity."

"My grandchildren will be the first to benefit from the great method."

"How many can benefit?"

"Five at most. Three of yours, two of ours. As agreed."

The Blood Flame Sect Leader's gaze swept the chamber and stopped on a large drawing affixed to the middle of the wall. A diagram of a human body -- muscle structure, meridians, the twelve regular meridians and eight extraordinary vessels, all rendered in meticulous detail.

Among them, the Hundred Meetings point at the crown stood out. Noticeably wider than the rest. The old man's twisted grin spread as he noticed where the Sect Leader stared.

"Desolate Fortress' Lightning Genius."

His voice was barely a whisper. Mad with anticipation.

"That... is not like our bodies. It's truly wondrous. Even compared to your Blood Flame Sect or the rabble from the Elf tribe. I'm nearly mad with anticipation for the great method. The key lies there -- how well we can replicate that diagram's body."

"Not like our bodies?" The Seventh Apostle's voice carried a new heat. "How does it look to you?"

The old man's eyes gleamed in the dying light of the last pearl.

"With its supernatural strength and breathing techniques that reach divine methods..." He paused. Drew the dagger along the air above the diagram's crown -- the Hundred Meetings point, rendered wide as a river mouth.

"It's closer to a spirit pretending to be human."

The pearl went dark.
